title: List Methods Practice
instructions: Practice using list methods like append(), insert(), remove(), pop(), sort(), and more.
hints: Use while loop with remove() to remove all occurrences
Find correct position by comparing with each element
Rotate by popping from front and appending to back
Check 'if item not in unique' before adding

testCases.description: remove_all_occurrences should remove every instance of the value
testCases.validation: assert remove_all_occurrences([1, 2, 3, 2, 4, 2], 2) == [1, 3, 4], 'Should remove all 2s from list'

testCases.description: remove_all_occurrences should return unchanged list if value not present
testCases.validation: assert remove_all_occurrences([1, 3, 5], 2) == [1, 3, 5], 'Should return unchanged list when value not found'

testCases.description: insert_sorted should insert value at the correct position in a sorted list
insert_sorted should append value larger than all elements
testCases.validation: assert insert_sorted([1, 2, 3], 10) == [1, 2, 3, 10], 'insert_sorted should append 10 at end'

testCases.description: rotate_left should shift elements left by n positions
get_unique_sorted should return sorted list with no duplicates
testCases.validation: assert get_unique_sorted([5, 3, 1, 3, 2, 5]) == [1, 2, 3, 5], 'get_unique_sorted should remove duplicates and sort'

description: Practice using Python list methods including remove() to eliminate all occurrences, insert() to maintain sorted order, pop() and append() for rotation, and building new lists with duplicate filtering followed by sort(). These methods are core tools for in-place list manipulation and transformation.
